title: Evangelion makes it into IGNs top 100 animated series
display_title: Evangelion makes it into IGNs top 100 animated series
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion makes it into IGN's top 100 animated series             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri Jan 23, 2009 10:20 pm For those who don't know it, IGN is an awesome site, that covers games, TV series, movies, comics, music and so on. Recently, they started making a Top 100 animated series list, posting 10 winners each day starting from 100. And so, I started following the list in hope that Eva would eventually show up. Misato: Congratulations, we are number 10! Other noticeable anime that made it into the list were: Cowboy Bebop, Death Note and Fullmetal Alchemist. I missed Ghost in the Shell SAC, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Fri Jan 23, 2009 10:24 pm Number 1 anime on the list!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Jan 24, 2009 5:37 am Simpsons made #1?

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Jan 24, 2009 8:03 am Simpsons has been shit for over a decade now. It's like the racehorse that should've been put down, but is still being entered because it once won the grand national.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Jan 24, 2009 12:48 pm Yay, number 10. I'm happy that it's on a list like that because most people can't focus on something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 24, 2009 1:28 pm Despite Simpsons going down the tubes lately, it is remarkable it's been on the air for as many years as it has. Besides, I personally still think it's hilarious, it just hasn't had that emotional depth some of the earlier seasons had. And at least Eva's ON the list and not overlooked.

[]:             Quote Post by Deathmask » Sat Jan 24, 2009 1:48 pm This list makes me RAEEEGHGGGGGGHHHGGGGGG I've never even seen the Tick

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jan 24, 2009 4:17 pm You guys actually care about the content of a site that knows only how to lick the hands which feed it?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Jan 24, 2009 6:02 pm What's with all of the Simpson's related hate? It's still a good, show, for proof watch the episode where Homer's mother (spoiler alert) died . I'm also biased to the Simpsons because they mentioned a certain giant purple robot in "Thirty Minutes Over Tokyo".

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Jan 24, 2009 6:05 pm I like The Simpsons. Although the recent seasons and episodes have not been so good and the movie was rather disappointing. These days I'm so-so with The Simpsons. I think I like Futurama more than the Simpsons.

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Sat Jan 24, 2009 9:14 pm I've never really liked the Simpsons too, but you simply can't ignore something that has 20 seasons. Out of all cartoons out there it is easily the most iconic. BobBQ wrote: You guys actually care about the content of a site that knows only how to lick the hands which feed it?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 25, 2009 1:52 am HENRCAST wrote: I've never really liked the Simpsons too, but you simply can't ignore something that has 20 seasons. Out of all cartoons out there it is easily the most iconic.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Jan 25, 2009 9:04 am I just can't believe they put batman over looney tunes. Bugs bunny et al would have been number one on my list. I still like those old cartoons better than anything else out there. But I can understand why Simpsons was #1. That franchise is a monster that is syndicated all around the globe and is liked pretty much everywhere it is aired. Deathmask wrote: I've never even seen the Tick

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jan 25, 2009 4:46 pm While I am impressed that Evangelion made number 10 on that list, I am hesitant to call this cause for celebration, as it lends some credence to other TOP [insert random number] [insert medium/genre] of ALL TIME lists.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Jan 26, 2009 3:02 pm At least Batman: The Animated Series got the #2 position, and The Real Ghostbusters is on there at all, so I'm perfectly happy with it. Congrats on EVA making #10, though. I'm very surprised it's on there, and I'm amused to read the comments of IGN's members, who seem to miss the point!

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Jan 26, 2009 3:06 pm Yay, The Real Ghostbusters. I loved that show. Got the 2-disc DVD of the movies and there was a couple episodes on there.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Jan 26, 2009 3:47 pm It cool that it is #10, The Simpsons was great back in the 80's, 90's and early 00's but it got downhill since then. I like Family Guy and American Dad way better. The Guyver and TTGL should be on the list though.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:01 pm BobBQ wrote: You guys actually care about the content of a site that knows only how to lick the hands which feed it?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:46 pm I'm spirse the HBO Spawn series and 90's Hulk Cartoon are not on the list.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jan 27, 2009 12:33 pm they didn't pay enough lol
summary: []: Evangelion makes it into IGN's top 100 animated series             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri Jan 23, 2009 10:20 pm For those who don't know it, IGN is an awesome site, that covers games, TV series, movies, comics, music and so on. Recently, they started making a Top 100 animated series list, posting 10 winners each day starting from 100. And so, I started following the list in hope that Eva would eventually show up. Misato: Congr
